title: A computing platform system arranged for executing a plurality of processes and a method for handling processes in one of a plurality of connected computing platforms comprised in a computing platform system
abstract: A computing platform system comprising a plurality of inter- connected computing platforms, wherein each computing platform comprises at least one reconfigurable hardware device such as a Field Programmable Gate Array, FPGA, and at least one processor, such as a physical entity or implemented as a softcore, arranged for communicating with said reconfigurable hardware device.
claims:
Claims 1 . A computing platform system comprising a plurality of interconnected computing platforms, wherein each computing platform comprises: - at least one reconfigurable hardware device such as a Field Programmable Gate Array, FPGA; at least one processor, such as a physical entity or implemented as a softcore, arranged for communicating with said reconfigurable hardware device; an operating system arranged to be executed on said at least one processor and arranged for managing execution of at least one application comprising a plurality of processes, wherein said computing platform further comprises: a first programmed concurrent process execution frame work, comprised within said at least one reconfigurable hardware device, comprising of one or multiple pre-defined reconfigurable areas on said at least one reconfigurable hardware device and a routing infrastructure arranged to exchange data within said frame work, said computing platform further comprising a reconfigurable infrastructure arranged to re-program said reconfigurable areas; a library of re-locatable and instantaneously available user- defined hardware functions, wherein said hardware functions are compatible with said pre-defined reconfigurable areas in said concurrent execution frame work; a reconfiguring manager arranged for dynamically reconfiguring said reconfigurable hardware device at run-time based on processes to be executed and instantaneous available reconfigurable hardware device resources, wherein said reconfiguring being physically altering said reconfigurable hardware device resources by programming said hardware device; a task manager arranged for queue communicating with said reconfiguring manager and for scheduling said processes on either one of said at least one processor and said at least one reconfigurable hardware device, - an allocation table at least comprising said processes to be executed, wherein each process is coupled with a Quality-of-Service, QoS, parameter; wherein said task manager is further arranged for determining that a QoS parameter of one of said processes in said allocation table can not be met; 

 wherein each computing platform further comprises a process allocation manager arranged for: requesting any of said plurality of inter-connected computing platforms for taking over said process for which said QoS parameter can not be met; receiving an indication, by a of said plurality of inter-connected computing platforms, that said process having said QoS parameter can be taken over; exchanging said process for which said QoS parameter can not be met with said computing platform for which said indication has been received; receiving, from said computing platform for which said indication has been received, data output of said process that has been performed on said computing platform for which said indication has been received. 2. A computing platform system according to claim 1 , wherein said QoS parameter comprises any of: latency requirements for a corresponding process; jitter requirements for a corresponding process; throughput requirements for a corresponding process; synchronisation requirements for a corresponding process. 3. A computing platform system according to any of the previous claims, wherein said allocation table further comprises scheduled and exchanged processes, and wherein each process is coupled to a computing platform identifier identifying which computing platform of said plurality of computing platforms is responsible for processing each process, and wherein each process is coupled to an application identifier identifying which application is responsible for each process. 4. A computing platform system according to any of the previous claims, wherein said plurality of computing platforms are inter-connected via a communication network. 5. A computing platform system according to claim 4, wherein each computing platform comprises: 

 network manager arranged for issuing a discovery message to said plurality of inter-connected computing platforms for revealing its presence in said communication network. 6. A method for handling processes in one of a plurality of interconnected computing platforms comprised in a computing platform system according to any of the previous claims, wherein said method comprises, performed by said one of said plurality of inter-connected computing platforms, the steps of: determining, by said task manager, that a QoS parameter of one of said processes to be executed in said allocation table can not be met; requesting, by said process allocation manager, any of said plurality of inter-connected computing platforms for taking over said process for which said QoS parameter can not be met; receiving, by said process allocation manager, an indication, by a of said plurality of inter-connected computing platforms, that said process having said QoS parameter can be taken over, and exchanging, by said process allocation manager, said process for which said QoS parameter can not be met with said computing platform for which said indication has been received, and - receiving, by said process allocation manager, from said computing platform for which said indication has been received, data output of said process that has been performed on said computing platform for which said indication has been received 7. A method for handling processes according to claim 6, wherein said QoS parameter comprises any of: latency requirements for a corresponding process; jitter requirements for a corresponding process; throughput requirements for a corresponding process; synchronisation requirements for a corresponding process. 8. A method for handling processes according to any of the claims 6 - 7, wherein said allocation table further comprises scheduled and exchanged processes, and wherein each process is coupled to a computing platform identifier 

 identifying which computing platform of said plurality of computing platforms is responsible for processing each process. 9. A method for handling processes according to any of the claims 6 - 8, wherein said plurality of computing platforms are inter-connected via a communication network. 10. A method for handling processes according to any of the claims 6 - 9 in combination with claim 5, said method comprises the step of: - issuing, by said network manager, a discovery message to said plurality of inter-connected computing platforms for revealing its presence in said communication network. 1 1 . A method for handling processes according to any of the claims 6 - 9 in combination with claim 5, said method comprises the step of: issuing, by said network manager, a status message to said plurality of inter-connected computing platforms for identifying the status of the computing platform in said communication network.
